creator: FreeBSD@ShaneWare.Biz
text:
Created attachment 179351
update for graphics/openshadinglanguage

Upstream is enabling C++11 by default - looks like that may only be in 1.8.x. I deliberately disabled it after testing and finding that it breaks linking with boost. C++14 was just me keeping the two together.

The inclusion of clang34 is only used with src/liboslexec/llvm_ops.cpp the rest is built using CXX. USES=compiler was to prevent gcc42 being used, the powerpc maintainers added USE_GCC to fix powerpc builds - see ports r402595. That may still be required to force use of gcc49 over gcc42? or would `USES=compiler:c++11-lib` be a better way? I can't test powerpc builds here.
